Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First, lets recall the typical sequence for a Hire-to-Retire process: 1. Post Job Ad 2. Screen Candidates 3. Conduct Interviews 4. Make Hiring Decision 5. Onboard Employee 6. Add to Payroll 7. Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now, lets analyze Model 1: Model 1 structure: - Nodes: Post, Screen, Decide, Interview, Onboard, Payroll, Close - Order relations: - Post  Screen - Screen  Decide - Screen  Interview - Decide  Onboard - Onboard  Payroll - Payroll  Close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies in Model 1: 1. The main issue is the relationship between Screen, Interview, and Decide. Normally, you would screen candidates, then interview the selected ones, then make a decision. However, in this model: - Screen  Decide: This suggests you can make a hiring decision immediately after screening, without conducting interviews.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means Decide can happen immediately after Screen, or Interview can happen after Screen, but theres no requirement that Decide happens after Interview. ... This is a significant anomaly because it allows making hiring decisions before or without conducting interviews.

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: For example: Screen  Decide  Onboard  Payroll  Close and separately: Screen  Interview This would mean you onboard someone before interviewing them, which is illogical.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now, lets analyze Model 2: Model 2 structure: ... Order relations: - Post  Screen - Post  Interview - Interview  Decide - Decide  loop_onboarding - loop_onboarding  xor_payroll - xor_payroll  Close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies in Model 2: 1. Post  Interview: This means you can go directly from posting the job ad to conducting interviews without screening candidates. ... 4. The XOR on payroll: ... Skipping payroll would be abnormal

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: There is no edge from Screen to Decide or Screen to anything else except that its a node. So Screen is only connected from Post, and has no outgoing edges. This means that Screen can happen, but its not connected to the rest of the process.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The normative process has the following dependencies: 1. Post before Screen 2. Screen before Interview 3. Interview before Decide 4. Decide before Onboard 5. Onboard before Payroll 6. Payroll before Close Now, lets see which model enforces more of these:

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 enforces 5 out of 6 normative dependencies (missing Interview  Decide), but it also enforces an extra dependency (Screen  Decide) that allows bypassing Interview.

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: So possible orderings include: 1. Correct: Post, Screen, Interview, Decide, Onboard, Payroll, Close 2. Incorrect: Post, Screen, Decide, Onboard, Payroll, Close, Interview ... 4. Post, Screen, Decide, Onboard, Interview, Payroll, Close

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Therefore, despite its ordering anomalies, Model 1 is more correct because it ensures all activities are performed, whereas Model 2 allows skipping critical activities like Payroll.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Therefore, Model 1 more closely aligns with a normative Hire-to-Retire process.
